ABSTRACT KEYWORDS
Understanding the determination mechanism and dynamics of shipping Ocean shipping; freight rate;
freight rates has significant implications for practitioners, economists, and quantitative modelling; time
regulators of maritime transport.

children.title: MARITIME POLICY & MANAGEMENT 5
children.summary:
Number of papers per research objectives. The categorization is based on two kinds of dynamics in freight rates: one is temporal dynamics,
containing time-based patterns and trends (related to objective groups (1), (2), and (3)); and the second
is cross-sectional dynamics that describes changes in freight rates caused by external factors irrespective
of time (related to objective groups (4)).

children.title: MARITIME POLICY & MANAGEMENT 7
children.summary:

cyclic behaviour, volatility cluster, and stationarity) of freight rates, as they have the advantage of
being unlimited by linearity and stationarity. Forecasting papers predominantly focused on the freight rate temporal dynamics in the dry bulk
and tanker sectors, most often using the dataset published by the Baltic Exchange.

children.title: MARITIME POLICY & MANAGEMENT 9
children.summary:
Most papers studied freight rates of dry bulk (62.5%) and tanker (29.2%), with only 13.1% on
container. However, when only looking at the papers with multivariate models, the percentage
share of papers on dry bulk decreases to 58.6%, while the percentage share of papers on tanker
increases slightly to 29.3% and container to 17.2%.

children.title: MARITIME POLICY & MANAGEMENT 11

mid-2020. Besides, US dollar exchange rate could affect container freight rates, as currency adjust-

dollar and other currencies.

children.title: MARITIME POLICY & MANAGEMENT 13
children.summary: Co-authorship network (Nodes and edges are weighted according to the frequency of appearance). Overall, the authorship network is widely dispersed with 1.64 papers/author on average.

children.title: MARITIME POLICY & MANAGEMENT 15
children.summary:
Trends and achievements
Only 22 papers (13.1% of the shipping freight rate papers) in the past 20 years studied container
freight rates, 21 of which investigated the container liner shipping freight rates charged from
shippers and the remaining one studied containership charter rates and the correlations among
different shipping sectors. In terms of research objectives, more focus was given to temporal dynamics compared to cross-
sectional dynamics caused by micro-level factors (e.g., vessel characteristics, contract terms).
